name: Neutral and ground are always separate
statement: Neutral and ground are never connected — they are kept fully separate throughout the system.
correction: Neutral and ground ARE bonded — just at exactly one point: the service disconnect. That single bond is what makes a ground fault clear: when a hot wire touches a metal case, current flows hot → EGC → main bond → neutral → back to the utility transformer, fast and high enough to trip the breaker. With no bond at all, a fault would energize the case and sit there waiting for someone to touch it.
